title: koeleria cristata subsp. montana
intro: Koeleria cristata subsp. montana, also known as mountain June grass, is a perennial grass species native to mountainous regions. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Koeleria cristata subsp. montana can be used in herbal preparations, often in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
